requirement: UI Dashboard auto refresh time incorrect, 2 appears to be 2 millisecond, not 2 second.

tasks.description:
I'll also use `glob` to find frontend files (likely `.py` if it's a Python backend with a frontend, or `.js`, `.ts`, `.html` if it's a web app, or maybe a Qt/GUI app).
Given the project name "spine" and the path, it might be a Python project.

feature_slices.description: Implement explicit unit conversion logic in the configuration layer to transform the raw numeric interval value (2) into the expected duration (2000ms), replacing any implicit or missing multipliers.
acceptance: Raw configuration value '2' is deterministically converted to 2000ms before reaching runtime logic
Conversion logic includes validation to reject or normalize invalid/negative time inputs

feature_slices.description: Refactor the Dashboard component and associated hooks to consume the corrected interval, ensuring timers are properly initialized, paused/resumed with state changes, and safely cleared on unmount to prevent race conditions or memory leaks.
acceptance: Dashboard data refreshes trigger exactly once every 2 seconds when active
Timer instances are fully cleaned up on component unmount or when refresh is disabled, with no dangling promises or intervals

feature_slices.description: Develop automated integration and performance tests to verify the corrected refresh timing, validate that downstream data fetching and UI rendering pipelines remain stable, and confirm no regressions in adjacent dashboard modules.
acceptance: Tests assert refresh intervals align with 2000ms ± acceptable tolerance across multiple cycles
No excessive API calls or UI thrashing observed under continuous refresh load
Core dashboard features (data display, navigation, manual refresh) function identically post-deployment
